channel.title: SpotPulse Trending
channel.description: Live web pulse — India & Global intelligence dashboard.

item.title: Iran war could prompt Federal Reserve to raise rates, Pimco says - Financial Times
item.description: Iran war could prompt Federal Reserve to raise rates, Pimco says Financial Times

item.title: Ganesh Kumar alleges UDF-BJP deal in Pathanapuram
item.description: Ganesh Kumar pointed to a significant and ‘suspicious’ drop in the BJP’s vote share as primary evidence of cross-voting intended to consolidate the anti-LDF front

item.title: India vs Pakistan: If war breaks out, who actually has their back? - Zee News
item.description: India vs Pakistan: If war breaks out, who actually has their back? Zee News

item.title: Iraq Never Knew Its Desert Was Hiding An Israeli Military Base
item.description: US Army aviation officials inspect the wreckage of an Apache AH-64D attack helicopter. (Representational)

item.title: Ollama Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability Allows Remote Process Memory Leak
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security vulnerability in Ollama that, if successfully exploited, could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to leak its entire process memory. The out-of-bounds read flaw, which likely impacts over 300,000 servers globally, is tracked as CVE-2026-7482 (CVSS score: 9.1). It has been codenamed Bleeding Llama by Cyera. Ollama is a

item.title: Air India has terminated over 1,000 staff in last 3 years for ethical breaches: CEO
item.description: Flagging that hundreds of employees are terminated every year due to non-compliance, Mr. Wilson has also emphasised that the staff need to act in the right way even when others are not looking

item.title: Bogota International Book Fair 2026: India As Guest Of Honour Sparks Colombia Craze
item.description: FILBo 2026: The event also saw strong engagement from children and students.

item.title: ChatGPT's New 'Duty To Warn': Why Your AI Confessor Is No Longer Private
item.description: OpenAI's Trusted Contact alerts a real person if ChatGPT detects severe distress or self-harm risk

item.title: SK Hynix flooded with unprecedented offers from big tech firms to secure chip supplies
item.description: Global tech giants are making unprecedented offers to SK Hynix. They want to invest in new production lines and fund expensive equipment purchases. This is to secure vital memory chips. Demand for these chips is surging due to the artificial intelligence boom. SK Hynix is cautious about these deals. The company is reviewing various approaches to secure long-term supply agreements.

item.title: PM Modi assures more support to Telangana, asks CM Revanth to collaborate with him
item.description: PM Narendra Modi inaugurates or lays foundation stone for Zaheerabad Industrial Park (National Investment and Manufacuting Zone), IOC terminal at Malkapur, multi-tracking of railway line between Kazipet and Vijayawada and PM Mitra Park in Warangal

item.title: ‘Need of the hour to use petrol, gas, diesel with great restraint’: PM Modi on mitigating Middle East war impact
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi called for careful use of petrol, diesel, and gas. He stated that reducing reliance on imported fuel will help India manage the ongoing war's effects. The Prime Minister also highlighted India's focus on energy security and investments in solar power. Several infrastructure projects worth around Rs 9,400 crore were inaugurated in Telangana.

item.title: LVPEI organises Whitathon in Hyderabad to raise awareness on childhood eye cancer
Can central banks curb inflation as energy costs rise?
item.description: Central banks hold rates steady as energy shock tests inflation fight.

item.title: Putin’s angry warning after Trump-backed Truce gets ignored, Ukraine hijacks May 9 ceasefire offer | Videos - Hindustan Times
item.description: Putin’s angry warning after Trump-backed Truce gets ignored, Ukraine hijacks May 9 ceasefire offer | Videos Hindustan Times

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire under strain as Gulf states report drone attacks
item.description: Drone hits cargo ship off Qatar’s coast, while Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates repel attacks.

item.title: Logitech bets on AI, gaming and business users as it raises spending, CEO says
item.description: Logitech plans increased spending on product development and marketing this year. The company is focusing on gaming, business customers, and AI-enabled devices for growth. Despite economic slowdown worries and Middle East supply issues impacting sales, Logitech expects continued momentum. Investments will be made to capitalize on AI opportunities and maintain a strong financial base.

item.title: Nutritionist Explains How A Planned Cheat Meal Can Actually Prevent Metabolic Slowdown
item.description: A nutritionist explains how one planned cheat meal can support consistency, reduce cravings and make weight loss more sustainable.

item.title: Modi's 'need of the hour' message amid West Asia oil crisis: ‘Use petrol, gas, diesel with great restraint’
item.description: Modi's remarks come as India grapples with an energy crunch triggered by the ongoing US-Iran war, which has disrupted supplies following a blockade of Hormuz

item.title: The idea that Claude has feelings is great for Anthropic: Parmy Olson
item.description: Richard Dawkins, a prominent skeptic, recently declared a large language model named Claude to be conscious after a series of interactions. This experience highlights how AI's mimicked empathy can lead to human attachment, a commercially valuable trait for tech companies.

item.title: "Open Hooliganism": Punjab BJP Slams AAP After Alleged Attack On Leaders
item.description: The BJP leader further alleged that police failed to take action against those involved

item.title: Anthropic signs $1.8 billion AI cloud deal with Akamai, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: AI startup Anthropic has secured a significant computing deal worth $1.8 billion with Akamai Technologies. This partnership aims to meet the growing demand for Anthropic's AI software. Akamai's shares saw a substantial rise following the announcement. The company is well-positioned to access necessary components for AI development. This development follows Anthropic's recent computing deal with Elon Musk's SpaceX.

item.title: ‘Amazon of America’: film paints vision of a post-coup Brazil giving up rainforest
item.description: Vitória Régia imagines rightwing Bolsonaro plot succeeded with US help – and highlights threats facing Indigenous peoples The year is 2025 and far-right coup plotters have annihilated Brazil’s democracy, assassinating the president, closing the national congress and surrendering the Amazon rainforest and its untold riches to the United States. “Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the Amazon of America,” a thick-accented

item.title: Iran war ceasefire tested as cargo ship catches fire and Kuwait, UAE repel drone attacks - Spectrum News
item.description: Iran war ceasefire tested as cargo ship catches fire and Kuwait, UAE repel drone attacks Spectrum News

item.title: Indian mangoes for trophy: Singapore festival rewards performers with fruit as India pushes exports
item.description: Singapore's cultural festival saw Indian mangoes as the star attraction, with performers receiving boxes of the prized fruit. Organized by the Indian High Commission, the event aimed to boost mango exports and offered a taste of "Incredible India." This initiative, part of a broader strategy to promote premium varieties globally, has generated significant enthusiasm and curiosity among residents.

item.title: 'Using Fuel With Restraint Is Need Of Hour': PM Modi Says India Committed Towards Energy Security
item.description: The Prime Minister underscored the need for restraint in using energy imports to conserve reserves and counter the adverse impact of the ongoing war.

item.title: US-Iran conflict: Unidentified projectile hits cargo vessel near Qatar, drones breach Kuwaiti, UAE airspaces
item.description: Authorities in Qatar and Kuwait said no injuries resulted from the strikes. These incidents represent the newest risks to a month-old cessation of hostilities, which the Trump administration maintains is still functional

item.title: Asim Munir At His Lying Best On Anniversary Of Pakistan’s Greatest Military Debacle In Recent Times; Claims India Sought Ceasefire - OdishaBytes
item.description: Asim Munir At His Lying Best On Anniversary Of Pakistan’s Greatest Military Debacle In Recent Times; Claims India Sought Ceasefire OdishaBytes

item.title: Security alert issued after gelatin sticks found near PM Modi's event venue in Bengaluru
item.description: At least two gelatin sticks were found on the side of the footpath, nearly 3 kilometres away from the main venue in Bengaluru on Sunday, where Prime Minister Narendra Modi was scheduled to arrive today, police said.

item.title: Scientists successfully transfer longevity gene and extend lifespan
item.description: Scientists at the University of Rochester pulled off a remarkable experiment: they transferred a longevity-related gene from the famously long-lived naked mole rat into mice, and the mice ended up healthier and lived longer. The special gene boosts production of a substance called high molecular weight hyaluronic acid, which appears to protect against cancer, reduce inflammation, and support healthier aging. The modi

item.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Monday
item.description: Domestic equity benchmarks ended sharply lower on Friday amid rising geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran, rupee weakness and heavy profit-booking in financial stocks. Analysts said the Nifty slipping below key technical levels signals weakening momentum, although select indicators still point towards the possibility of a near-term recovery.

item.title: Miscreants loot idols, vandalise Hanuman temple in Shillong; set fire to CCTV cameras before fleeing
item.description: The incident occurred around 1 am. Police said the thieves stole idols of Lord Krishna and Radha, along with a smaller idol of Lord Hanuman.

item.title: Iran war day 72: Tehran yet to reply to US plan; Israel bombs Lebanon - Al Jazeera
item.description: Iran war day 72: Tehran yet to reply to US plan; Israel bombs Lebanon Al Jazeera

item.title: ’30 runs at strike rate of 300 but…’: Hayden on what Sooryavanshi lacked vs GT
How much is the Iran war affecting US inflation?
item.description: Market Questions is the FT’s guide to the week ahead

item.title: Court Ruling Against Trump Tariffs Unlikely to Lower Prices Amid Iran War | 2026 - News and Statistics - IndexBox
item.description: Court Ruling Against Trump Tariffs Unlikely to Lower Prices Amid Iran War | 2026 - News and Statistics IndexBox

item.title: Could Contact-Tracing Apps Help With the Hantavirus? Not Really
item.description: Contact-tracing apps were widely deployed during the Covid pandemic. They aren’t as helpful during smaller outbreaks.

item.title: Spain starts evacuating virus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
item.description: Spanish passengers are the first to leave the MV Hondius, after an outbreak saw three people die and several infected.

item.title: Why is Iran taking time to respond to the US proposal to end the war?
item.description: Iran continues to review the proposal as Tehran's nuclear programme and Strait of Hormuz remain main sticking points.

item.title: Delhi on high alert: Intel agencies warn of terror attacks; security beefed up at BJP HQ - MSN
item.description: Delhi on high alert: Intel agencies warn of terror attacks; security beefed up at BJP HQ MSN

item.title: PM Modi rolls out Rs 9,400 crore Telangana package, spotlights textile revolution
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched infrastructure and industrial projects worth nearly Rs 9,400 crore in Telangana. He inaugurated the PM MITRA Park in Warangal, the country's first fully operational textile park. The projects aim to boost manufacturing and connectivity, with a focus on the textile sector. Several road, railway, and logistics projects were also unveiled.
